findings.title: No baseline-model code in repo; Table 1/2 competitor numbers cannot be reproduced
findings.quote:
# Function to save results to CSV
def save_results(df_results, csv_file_path, result_row):
    row_count = len(df_results) + 1
    df_results.loc[row_count] = result_row
    df_results.to_csv(csv_file_path, index=False)
findings.claim: The evaluation scripts only generate, evaluate, and save SDForger's own results; no implementation, wrapper, config, or driver exists for any of the five baselines (TimeVAE, TimeVQVAE, RTSGAN, SDEGAN, LS4) that Tables 1 and 2 compare against.
findings.concern: Every comparative claim in Tables 1-2 (and D.3/D.4/D.10-D.14) depends on baseline numbers that no code in the package produces, so the central 'outperforms existing generative models' conclusion is not reproducible from the artefact.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the baseline-generation scripts/configs (or pinned forks) and a driver that produces every baseline row under the same split, scaling, and metric pipeline as SDForger.
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at the cited lines; a repo-wide search for TimeVAE, TimeVQVAE, RTSGAN, SDEGAN, and LS4 returns zero hits in sources/ and utils/, confirming no baseline implementation exists.

findings.title: No driver to run the 12-dataset aggregation behind Table 1 and the appendix tables
findings.quote:
if __name__ == "__main__":

    # Set paths for output, model, and data input
    OUTPUT_PATH = os.path.join('output')
    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_PATH, exist_ok=True)

    # Set output results file
    # csv_file_path = os.path.join(str(OUTPUT_PATH), data + '_TSG_evaluation.csv')
    csv_file_path = os.path.join(str(OUTPUT_PATH), 'TSG_evaluation.csv')
    df_results = initialize_results_file_tsg(csv_file_path)
findings.claim: Each script run evaluates a single config (one dataset, one setting). Only the ecl (multisample) and bikesharing (univariate/multivariate) configs are shipped; there is no loop or batch script over the 12 datasets, settings, embedding dims, and seeds that the aggregated Table 1 and appendix Tables D.3/D.4/D.10-D.14 summarise.
findings.concern: The aggregated and per-dataset numbers cannot be regenerated without the user re-deriving 12+ dataset configurations and the averaging/normalisation/ranking logic, none of which is provided.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the per-dataset configs (data_name, channels, lengths, k) and the aggregation/normalisation/ranking script that turns per-run CSVs into Table 1 and the appendix tables.
findings.paper_ref: Table 1; Appendix Tables D.3, D.4, D.10-D.14
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at the cited lines; sources/config/ contains only config.yaml (bikesharing univariate) and config_ttm.yaml with no loop or batch driver, confirming no multi-dataset harness exists.

findings.title: Figure-2 conditional-generation accuracy (0.81) has no computing code
findings.claim: The conditional_generation.ipynb notebook performs text-conditioned generation and plotting (the only artefact for Section 6), but contains no longitudinal kNN classifier, no accuracy computation, and no scikit-fda usage; a static scan for KNeighbors/accuracy_score/skfda/0.81 across all notebooks and scripts returns nothing.
findings.concern: The Section-6 headline number 'accuracy of 0.81 in identifying the generated curves' cannot be reproduced because the classifier that produces it is absent from the package.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the longitudinal kNN classification script (training data, class labels, fit/predict, accuracy) that produces the 0.81 figure.
findings.reason: Paper_text.txt confirms the 0.81 accuracy claim (line 940); a search of all code cells in conditional_generation.ipynb and all .py files in sources/ and utils/ finds no KNeighbors, accuracy_score, or skfda usage, confirming the classifier code is absent.

findings.title: SHAP-RE reshape assumes single channel; crashes for any C>1 input
findings.quote:
train_data  = orig_data.reshape(orig_data.shape[0], orig_data.shape[1])
test_data = gen_data.reshape(gen_data.shape[0], gen_data.shape[1])
train_y  = np.random.rand(orig_data.shape[0])
test_y = np.random.rand(gen_data.shape[0])
n_train, p = train_data.shape
findings.claim: calculate_shapelet_recons_err reshapes a (n_samples, length, n_channels) array to (n_samples, length), which is only size-consistent when n_channels==1; for C>1 numpy raises 'cannot reshape array of size ... into shape (n,length)'.
findings.concern: The SHAP-RE column is reported in Table 1 only for the single-channel similarity settings, so the reported numbers are unaffected, but the metric silently cannot be applied to multivariate data and would crash if used there.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm SHAP-RE was computed per channel for single-channel data only, or generalise the reshape to handle C>1 (e.g. flatten or loop over channels).
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at lines 20-24; reshape to (n_samples, length) is indeed size-inconsistent for C>1 input arrays of shape (n_samples, length, n_channels), confirming the crash claim.

findings.title: Shipped univariate config uses k=5 and L0=2000-but-1-sample, paper says k=3
findings.quote:
data_train_channels: 
- cnt
data_train_params:
- 2000
- 1
evaluation:
  generated_data_path: output/generated_data.npy
  train_data_path: output/train_data.npy
save_results: true
sdforger_augmentation_strategy: univariate
sdforger_batch: 32
sdforger_embedding_dim: 5
findings.claim: The default univariate example config sets sdforger_embedding_dim: 5, whereas the paper fixes k=3 for the multisample and univariate settings (Section 4 'Parameter settings'). The repo's README is consistent with the paper text (k=3), so the example config differs from the paper's reported setting.
findings.concern: A user running the shipped config out-of-the-box reproduces a k=5 run, not the k=3 setting that the paper's main univariate results use; the discrepancy is a config default, and the code path supports k=3 via the same field.
findings.resolution: Authors: set the example config defaults to the paper's reported k (3) for the main settings, or document that the shipped configs are illustrative rather than the exact Table-1 settings.
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at lines 4-14 of config.yaml; sdforger_embedding_dim is 5 in the shipped config, confirmed as a discrepancy from the paper's k=3.

findings.title: TTM RMSE/MASE/WQL averaged over all forecast channels, not just the target
findings.quote:
for sample in range(0, true_val.shape[0]):
      list_rmse_per_channel = []
      list_mase_per_channel = []
      list_wql_per_channel = []
      list_h1_per_channel = []
      for channel in range(0, pred_val.shape[2]):
          rmse = compute_rmse(true_val[sample, :, channel], pred_val[sample, :, channel])
          mase = compute_mase(np.array(true_val[sample, :, channel]), np.array(pred_val[sample, :, channel]))
          wql = compute_wql(np.array(true_val[sample, :, channel]), np.array(pred_val[sample, :, channel]))
          h1 = compute_h1_distance(np.array(true_val[sample, :, channel]), np.array(pred_val[sample, :, channel]))
          list_rmse_per_channel.append(rmse)
          list_mase_per_channel.append(mase)
          list_wql_per_channel.append(wql)
          list_h1_per_channel.append(h1)
      rmse_list.append(list_rmse_per_channel)
      mase_list.append(list_mase_per_channel)
      wql_list.append(list_wql_per_channel)
      h1_list.append(list_h1_list)

  # Step 7: Calculate the average metrics across all windows
  avg_rmse = np.mean(rmse_list)
findings.claim: evaluate_ttm_model loops over every channel in pred_val (range(0, pred_val.shape[2])) and averages the per-channel RMSE/MASE/WQL uniformly, whereas Table 2 frames the task as forecasting a single target (e.g. bikesharing target: count, controls: temperature, humidity).
findings.concern: If pred_val carries all input channels (target + controls), the reported per-dataset metric mixes the target's error with control-channel errors, so the Table-2 numbers would not be the pure target-forecast metric the caption implies; whether pred_val.shape[2]==1 (target-only) depends on tsfm internals I could not execute, hence a question.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether pred_val contains only the target channel or all channels; if all channels, restrict the metric to prediction_channel_indices (the target) so it matches the Table-2 caption.
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at lines 335-355; the channel loop over pred_val.shape[2] is confirmed; question status is appropriate since whether pred_val carries target-only or all channels cannot be verified without running tsfm.
